chapters.title: Introduction, Output and Math Operators
chapters.description: Python programs covering print statements and basic math operations.

problems.name: Print Coding Chef
problems.description: Write a Python program that prints the exact text `'Coding Chef'` to the console.
problems.constraints:
Time Limit: 1.0s
Memory Limit: 256MB
problems.starterCode:
# Write your code below
print("")

problems.name: Print difference of 10 and 3
problems.description: Write a program that calculates and prints the result of subtracting `3` from `10`.
problems.constraints: Do not just print the number 7; calculate it using mathematical operators.
problems.starterCode: # Calculate and print 10 - 3

problems.name: Print 6 divided by 2
problems.description: Write a program that divides `6` by `2` and prints the result. In Python, division returns a float, so the output should be `3.0`.
problems.constraints: Use division operator `/`.
problems.starterCode: # Divide 6 by 2 and print

problems.name: Print 5 5
problems.description: Write a Python program that prints the numbers `5` and `5` separated by a space.
problems.constraints: Do not print the output on separate lines.
problems.starterCode: # Print 5 and 5 separated by a space

problems.name: Print Multiple Strings
problems.description: Write a Python program to print 'Hello' and 'Python' separated by a space using a single print statement.
problems.constraints: Use a single print statement with two arguments separated by a comma.
problems.starterCode: # Print Hello and Python

problems.name: Print 108 using 9 and 12
problems.description: Write a Python program to print 108 using 9 and 12.
problems.constraints: Use the multiplication operator (*). Do not directly print 108.
problems.starterCode: # Multiply 9 and 12

problems.name: Print Squares
problems.description: Print the square values of 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5, each on a new line.
problems.constraints: Use exponentiation operator ** to compute squares.
problems.starterCode: # Write your code below

problems.name: Print Pattern
problems.description: Write a Python program to print the following pattern.
problems.constraints: Use only print() statements. Do not use loops.
problems.starterCode: # Write your code below

problems.name: Print Sum of 15 and 25
problems.description: Write a Python program that calculates and prints the sum of `15` and `25` using the addition operator `(+)`.
problems.constraints: Use the addition operator `(+)`. Do not directly print the answer.
problems.starterCode: # Calculate and print the sum

problems.name: Print Quotient and Remainder
problems.description: Write a Python program that calculates and prints the quotient and remainder when `10` is divided by `3`. Print each result on a new line.
problems.constraints: Use the floor division operator (//) and modulus operator (%). Do not directly print the answers.
problems.starterCode: # Calculate and print the quotient and remainder

chapters.title: Variables and Data Types
chapters.description: Exercises on using variables and working with different data types in Python.

problems.name: Variable Assignment
problems.description: Assign the value `25` to a variable named `age` and print it.
problems.constraints: Variable must be named `age`.
problems.starterCode: # Assign 25 to age and print age

problems.name: Print Name Using Variable
problems.description: Assign the value 'PythonGlow' to a variable named platform and print it.
problems.constraints: Variable must be named `platform`.
problems.starterCode: # Assign 'PythonGlow' to platform and print it

problems.name: Add Two Variables
problems.description: Assign `15` to variable `a` and `25` to variable `b`. Print the sum of `a` and `b`.
problems.constraints: Use variables to calculate the sum.
problems.starterCode:
# Assign values to a and b
# Print their sum

problems.name: Swap Two Variables
problems.description: Assign `10` to variable `a` and `20` to variable `b`. Swap their values and print `a` and `b` on separate lines.
problems.constraints: Use Python's variable swapping.
problems.starterCode:
# Assign values to a and b
# Swap them

problems.name: Store and Print Float
problems.description: Assign the value 3.14 to a variable named pi and print it.
problems.constraints: The variable must be named 'pi'.
problems.starterCode: # Assign 3.14 to pi and print it

problems.name: Store and Print Boolean
problems.description: Assign the value True to a variable named is_student and print it.
problems.constraints: The variable must be named 'is_student'.
problems.starterCode: # Assign True to is_student and print it

problems.name: Print Variable Data Types
problems.description:
Create the following variables:

name = "Python"
age = 20
height = 5.8
is_student = True

Print the data type of each variable using the type() function.
problems.constraints: Print each data type on a new line in the same order.
problems.starterCode: # Create the variables and print their data types

problems.name: Convert Integer to Float
problems.description: Assign the value 25 to a variable named num. Convert it to a float using the float() function and print the result.
problems.constraints: Use the float() function.
problems.starterCode: # Convert integer to float and print it

problems.name: Area of Rectangle using Variables
problems.description: Assign the values `12` and `8` to variables length and width. Calculate and print the area of the rectangle.
problems.constraints: Use variables to calculate the area.
problems.starterCode:
# Assign values to length and width
# Calculate the area

problems.name: Calculate Simple Interest
problems.description:
Assign the following values:

principal = 5000
rate = 8
time = 2

Calculate the simple interest using the formula:

SI = (principal * rate * time) / 100

Print the calculated simple interest.
problems.constraints: Use variables for all calculations. Do not directly print the answer.
problems.starterCode: # Calculate the simple interest

chapters.title: Strings
chapters.description: Practice questions on string operations and character manipulation in Python.

problems.name: Extract Substring
problems.description: You are given a string `text = "PythonProgramming"`. Write a program to print the first 6 characters (i.e. `"Python"`).
problems.constraints: Slice the string from index 0 to 6.
problems.starterCode:
text = "PythonProgramming"
# Print the sliced string below

chapters.title: User inputs
chapters.description: Coding exercises to handle user input and process data in Python.

problems.name: Sum of Two Inputs
problems.description: Read two integers from standard input and print their sum. Use `input()` to read each number.
problems.constraints: Inputs are given on two separate lines.
problems.starterCode: # Read two numbers and print their sum

chapters.title: Conditional Statements
chapters.description: Problems on if-else statements and logical operations in Python.

problems.name: Odd or Even
problems.description: Read an integer from the input. Print `'Even'` if the number is even, and `'Odd'` if it is odd.
problems.constraints: Outputs are case sensitive. Make sure to print exactly 'Even' or 'Odd'.
problems.starterCode: # Read number and print Even or Odd

chapters.title: Loops
chapters.description: Practice using while loops, for loops, and loop control statements.

problems.name: Factorial Calculator
problems.description: Read an integer `N` from standard input and compute its factorial. Print the computed factorial.
problems.starterCode: # Read N and calculate its factorial

chapters.title: Lists
chapters.description: Coding questions on list manipulation and array operations in Python.

problems.name: Sum Elements in List
problems.description: Write a program that takes a space-separated string of integers from standard input, converts it into a list of integers, and prints the sum of all elements.
problems.constraints: Use split() to parse the space-separated integers.
problems.starterCode: # Read space-separated values, parse, and print sum

chapters.title: Functions
chapters.description: Practice problems on defining and using functions in Python programs.

problems.name: Is Prime Function
problems.description: Define a function `is_prime(n)` that returns `True` if `n` is prime, and `False` otherwise. The input will contain an integer. Print the result of calling `is_prime` with that integer.
problems.starterCode:
def is_prime(n):
    # Write logic here
    pass

val = int(input())
print(is_prime(val))
